body.title: Self-edit & hot-reload — gated, inertia-aware source modification (Igor)
body.summary: Igor can modify his own source code, gated by inertia, syntax-checked, logged, and auto-committed — a step toward the self-programming epic. Targets carry an inertia level (HIGH 0.90+ requires Akien/arbiter review; MEDIUM discuss-first; LOW freely improvable and hot-reloadable). The preferred edit is a targeted patch (old_string -> new_string), syntax-checked before it touches disk so a rejected change never lands; a full-file rewrite is the dangerous fallback. After a LOW-inertia edit, hot-reload re-imports the module (tool modules self-re-register); HIGH-inertia modules (brainstem, memory/models.py, memory/cortex.py, reasoners/base.py) require a restart instead.

key_concepts.name: inertia gate
key_concepts.what: HIGH (review/arbiter) / MEDIUM (discuss) / LOW (free + hot-reload); the edit refuses or escalates by target inertia

key_concepts.name: patch preferred over rewrite
key_concepts.what: patch_source_file (targeted, syntax-checked, fails on stale/ambiguous match) is preferred; full rewrite only when a patch can't express the change

key_concepts.name: syntax check before disk
key_concepts.what: rejected changes never touch the file; the original is left intact on a syntax error

key_concepts.name: hot-reload vs restart
key_concepts.what: LOW-inertia modules reload via importlib and self-re-register; dataclass/DB-proxy/base modules are reload-blocked and need a restart

body.status_notes: GATED OFF by default (IGOR_SELF_EDIT_ENABLED=false), mirroring the calving-gated-off precedent. The improver rack DEVICE (devices/improver/) is the eventual relocation target for self-modification but is currently a stub (device.py only) — do NOT point at it as live implementation. Inertia tier-to-file table in the source doc is illustrative, not exhaustive.
